mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate wage income and consumption capacity, which drives positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns as corporate sales expectations get repriced higher.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=142624K at t-1mo shows rebounding labor demand
failure_condition: Rank IC between month-over-month PAYEMS change and 20-day forward returns is below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or the mechanism produces negative mean returns in the top quartile of PAYEMS growth months.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.25%, any month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate signals a liquidity-fueled recovery where risk assets reprice sharply; this triggers positive S&P 500 20-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.09% at effective zero lower bound
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, falling UNRATE coincides with negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered months, or the conditioned Sharpe ratio is below 0.2 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth alone does not lift equities unless accompanied by rising industrial production (INDPRO), because demand without supply confirmation leads to margin compression rather than earnings expansion; the interaction of rising RSAFS and rising INDPRO drives positive S&P 500 60-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS=540915M and INDPRO=105.7 indicate concurrent demand-supply recovery
failure_condition: In months where both RSAFS and INDPRO increase month-over-month, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) or the joint signal underperforms the single-series RSAFS signal by more than 2% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) predicts positive S&P 500 5-day returns only in the early stage of a recovery when high unemployment indicates slack resources and rapid margin expansion; when unemployment is elevated the signal is active and directional.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 6%, positive month-over-month INDPRO changes do not produce positive average 5-day S&P 500 returns, or the signal Sharpe ratio is below 0.3 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in housing starts (HOUST) predict negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days because residential investment is a leading component of GDP and its contraction signals tightening financial conditions and slowing construction activity ahead.
failure_condition: Declines in HOUST are followed by positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 55% of months, or the signal's Rank IC is positive over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2% year-over-year in a near-zero federal funds rate environment causes negative S&P 500 5-day returns as sharply negative real discount rates and expected Fed repricing compress forward earnings multiples abruptly.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL=261.8 with FEDFUNDS=0.09% creates deeply negative real rates
regime_hint: zero lower bound with rising inflation
failure_condition: Months with CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.0% and FEDFUNDS < 0.25% do not produce negative average 5-day S&P 500 returns, or the hit rate of negative returns falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment (UNRATE) combined with a flat federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS unchanged near zero) signals a stagnating economy with no monetary policy response, producing negative S&P 500 60-day returns as earnings downgrade risk accumulates.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=6.7% with FEDFUNDS=0.09% leaves policy constrained at the zero bound
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month and FEDFUNDS is unchanged, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in a bivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth (GDPC1) above 2% annualized predicts positive S&P 500 20-day returns as the broadest measure of domestic output validates prior risk-asset optimism and reduces recession probability.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1=18596.5BQ3 reflects rebound from pandemic trough
failure_condition: Top-quartile GDPC1 growth realizations are followed by negative S&P 500 20-day average returns, or the Rank IC between GDPC1 changes and forward returns is below 0.02 over a 3-year window.
